claims.claim: As the radius of the ISC increased from 0.25 mm to 1.25 mm, the total heat energy generated increased from 4.1 kJ to 85.4 kJ, and the average discharge current increased from 0.257 A to 5.626 A.
claims.quote: As the short circuit size increased, the total heat energy increased from 4.1 kJ (r = 0.25 mm) to 85.4 kJ (r = 1.25 mm), and the average current also increased from 0.257 A (r = 0.25 mm) to 5.626 A (r = 1.25 mm).

claims.claim: When the ISC radius was 0.35 mm or greater in a 22 Ah pouch LIB cell, the local temperature of the ISC zone exceeded the thermal runaway trigger temperature of 150°C.
claims.quote: When the size of the short circuit was larger than 0.35 mm, T cell,max was relatively small, but T SZ,max exceeded the trigger point of thermal abuse. These findings demonstrate that to prevent thermal incidents, there must not be a hole with radius larger than 0.35 mm in the separators used in a 22 Ah pouch LIB cell.

claims.claim: With ISC area fixed at 1 mm², increasing the short circuit perimeter from 4 mm to 32.125 mm increased total heat generation from 19.3 kJ to 28.3 kJ, but decreased the peak ISC zone temperature from 228.7°C to 108.2°C.
claims.quote: As the short circuit perimeter increased from 4 mm to 32.125 mm and the short circuit resistance decreased, the total heat generation increased from 19.3 kJ to 28.3 kJ, and the time-averaged current increased from 1.23 A to 1.81 A. Although the low and narrow short circuits led to large currents and heat generation, the peak temperatures of the ISC zone, Tmax, SZ, were rather low... When the short circuit perimeter was 4.0 mm, Tmax, SZ was approximately 228.7℃, while for the short circuit perimeter of 32.125 mm, Tmax, SZ was 108.2℃.

claims.claim: The ISC zone temperature was lowest (130°C) when the short circuit was located at the center of the LIB cell, while TSZ,max exceeded 160°C when the short was located on the side, indicating that ISC location near the cell edge increases the risk of thermal runaway.
claims.quote: The maximum temperature of the ISC zone, TSZ,max, was lowest (130℃) when the short was located at the center of the LIB cell, while TSZ,max was higher than 160℃ when the short was located on the side.

claims.claim: In the 22 Ah pouch cell with a 0.5 mm radius ISC, approximately 91% of total heat generation occurred in the small ISC zone, and approximately 80% of total heat was generated at the cathode due to its low electrical conductivity of 10 S/m.
claims.quote: Most of the heat, 91% of the total heat generation, was generated in the small area of the ISC zone because the resistance of the short was relatively large and the discharge current was small... The amount of heat generated at the cathode by the passage of the short circuit current was approximately 3.8 W, and it accounted for more than 80% of the total heat generation. This was because the electrical conductivity of the cathode was significantly low at 10 S/m, which was approximately 10% of that of the anode.

claims.claim: The activation energy of the electrolyte global Arrhenius equation plays the most important role in affecting the delay time for thermal runaway among the seven global Arrhenius equations for NCM811PC.
claims.quote: For the seven global Arrhenius equations, the activation energy of electrolyte global equation plays the most important role in affecting the delay time for thermal runaway.

claims.claim: After the frequency factor A of electrolyte is increased by 5%, the activation energy Ea of electrolyte is decreased by 5%, and the reaction enthalpy change ΔH of electrolyte is increased, the reaction front propagation during thermal runaway of an NCM811PC cell with nail penetration can be successfully captured.
claims.quote: After the frequency factor A of electrolyte is increased by 5 %, the activation energy Ea of electrolyte is decreased by 5 %, and the reaction enthalpy change ΔH of electrolyte is increased, the simulated battery thermal runaway process is shown in Fig. 7 where the unit of the temperature is K. Fig. 7 clearly shows that the reaction front propagation during the thermal runaway process can be successfully captured.

claims.claim: Using the original Arrhenius kinetics parameters obtained from DSC experiments, the simulation of an NCM811PC cell with nail penetration showed no reaction front propagation and no thermal runaway, whereas experiments under this condition do show thermal runaway.
claims.quote: From Fig. 5 (bottom right), it is seen that not only is there no reaction front propagation, but the temperature of the nail is also decreased due to no heat addition. Experiments under this condition do show thermal runaway.

claims.claim: Compared to NCM811PC, NCM622PC has a slower heat release rate and lower mean temperature during thermal runaway, and at the same time point (around 7 s) the reaction front location of NCM622PC is behind that of NCM811PC.
claims.quote: Compared to NCM811PC, NCM622PC has slower heat release rate and lower mean temperature. At the same time (around 7 s), the reaction front location of NCM622PC is behind that of NCM811PC.

claims.claim: In the simulated venting gases of an NCM811PC pouch cell with nail penetration, CO, CO2, and H2O have large amounts, C2H4 and O2 have medium amounts, while CH4, C2H6, and H2 have small amounts.
claims.quote: In the venting chemical species, CO, CO2, and H2O have large amounts, C2H4 and O2 have medium amounts, while CH4, C2H6, and H2 have small amounts.

claims.claim: The SEI layer decomposes first and generates heat at a temperature of approximately 90°C, initiating the subsequent chemical reaction of the negative electrode.
claims.quote: The SEI layer is decomposed first because it generates heat at temperature of approximately 90 °C and initiates the subsequent chemical reaction Rne of the negative electrode

claims.claim: The electrolyte starts to decompose at temperatures exceeding 200°C and releases the greatest amount of exothermic heat among all electrochemical components.
claims.quote: the remaining electrolyte Re starts to decompose at temperatures exceeding 200 °C, and this process releases the greatest amount of exothermic heat

claims.claim: Electrolyte decomposition during thermal runaway elevates cell temperature more than 300°C within a few seconds.
claims.quote: This chemical reaction elevates the cell temperature more than 300 °C within a few seconds, resulting the maximum temperature because of the significant amount of exothermic heat during TR.

claims.claim: The NMC cell reached a higher maximum temperature of 488.4°C at approximately 1850 s during thermal runaway under oven test conditions, compared to the LFP cell which reached a maximum temperature of 345.7°C at approximately 3200 s.
claims.quote: temperature at the bottom side of the NMC cell reaches the maximum temperature of 488.4 °C at approximately 1850 s, corresponding to the chemical reaction of both positive electrode and electrolyte... Temperature for the bottom side of the LFP cell then exceeds that of the oven plate... finally reaches the maximum temperature of 345.7 °C at approximately 3200 s

claims.claim: Under CC discharge at 3C, the core temperature is 10°C higher than the surface temperature, indicating that fast charging protocol may induce significant thermal gradient in the cell.
claims.quote: the core temperature is 10 °C higher than the surface temperature under the CC discharge condition at 3C... This result demonstrates that fast charging protocol might induce significant thermal gradient in the cell.
